nativeRange.habitat: Intertidal to shallow subtidal rocky substrate, reef flats, seagrass meadows, and mangrove roots; generally found just below the low-tide line

avoidWith: dedicated herbivorous fish (tangs, rabbitfish) in small tanks that may graze the lightly-calcified fronds

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Golden Scroll / Brown Scroll Algae (Padina sanctae-crucis). Sole hobby-trade source for this specialty entry.

summary: A shallow-water brown macroalga (Dictyotaceae) forming concentrically-banded, fan-shaped scrolling fronds in gold, yellow, and brown tones with whitened calcified upper surfaces. A sibling to Blue Scroll (Padina sp.) with similar care needs.
careNotes:
Golden Scroll (Padina sanctae-crucis) is a tropical brown macroalga in the family Dictyotaceae, order Dictyotales — one of the few calcareous genera among brown algae. Its thallus grows as a thin, fan- or scroll-shaped frond with characteristic concentric banding, typical of the Padina genus. The upper surfaces are lightly calcified and whitened, while the lower portions display the characteristic gold, yellow, and brown coloration that gives this trade name its meaning; the coloration can appear richer under strong full-spectrum reef lighting. The type locality is St. Croix, US Virgin Islands; the species has a broad tropical distribution spanning the Caribbean, Gulf of Mexico, Atlantic coast of South America, and into the Indo-Pacific.

As a shallow intertidal species (typically 0–12 m depth), it tends to do best under medium to strong aquarium lighting; secondary sources (Reef Cleaners, marineplantbook.com) describe 'strong to intense' or 'moderate to high' light as preferable, somewhat higher than typical display macroalgae. Users experiencing slow decline may benefit from increasing light intensity. Growth rate has been documented as slow in specialty sources, consistent with the calcified, intertidal Dictyotaceae growth habit.

This species is lightly calcified — it deposits a thin layer of calcium carbonate on its fronds, which may contribute minimally to alkalinity and calcium demand in the tank; regular dosing or a calcium reactor is generally advised for reef systems. Fronds may occasionally be grazed by herbivorous tankmates; most standard reef fish and invertebrates tend to leave it undisturbed. Moderate to strong flow is generally recommended.

Source coverage note: Mosaic Macros is the primary hobby-trade source for this specialty form. Cross-verification was performed using Wikipedia, Reef Cleaners, and marineplantbook.com. Lighting level was set to 'medium' per manifest specification; however, two secondary sources suggest medium-high to high is preferable — reviewers may wish to adjust this to 'high'. Growth rate was set to 'slow' based on the marineplantbook.com specialty source, overriding the manifest default of 'medium'; this is consistent with calcareous Dictyotaceae biology. Care level was set to 'intermediate'; marineplantbook.com rates Padina as 'advanced' citing calcium requirements and lighting sensitivity — reviewers may wish to adjust upward. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is generally accomplished by fragmentation — separating healthy frond sections and attaching them to rockwork or a plug. The species also reproduces via sporulation (asexual spore production) in the wild, with spores dispersing via water movement to settle on hard substrates. Sexual reproduction via alternating gametophyte–sporophyte lifecycle (typical of brown algae) occurs naturally but has not been documented in hobby aquaria.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Primary hobby-trade source for this specialty form. Provides species identification (Padina sanctae-crucis), trade name (Golden Brown Scroll), morphology description (ruffled fan/scroll form, gold/yellow/brown coloration with white accents), and general reef compatibility context. No explicit water parameter data.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Padina sanctae-crucis
additional.notes: Used for taxonomic classification (Dictyotaceae / Dictyotales / Phaeophyceae), morphology (curled fanlike branches ~15 cm, calcified whitened upper surface), native range (tropical worldwide, Caribbean, Florida, Brazil, South Pacific), habitat (intertidal to ~10 m, rocky substrate, reef flats, seagrass meadows, mangrove roots), and reproduction (sexual + asexual sporulation).

additional.name: Reef Cleaners — Scroll Algae
additional.notes: Cross-reference for aquarium husbandry. States 'strong to intense' lighting required due to shallow native habitat; notes lightly calcified fronds susceptible to herbivore grazing; mentions habitat as submerged rocks and mangrove roots just below the low-tide line.

additional.name: Marine Plant Book — Padina
additional.notes: Cross-reference for care parameters. Lighting 'moderate to high'; temperature 78–86°F (25.6–30°C); max height 6 in (~15 cm); growth rate 'slow'; native range Gulf of Mexico/Caribbean/Atlantic at 3–40 ft (0–12 m); propagation by fragmentation and sporulation; nutrient uptake 'fair'; requires calcium supplementation; rated 'advanced' difficulty.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
